message:
fix(core): prevent integer overflow in coordinate noise hash function

This commit wraps multiplications in the coordinate_noise() hash function
to prevent potential integer overflow when computing terrain noise patterns.

## Issue

**crates/scriptbots-core/src/lib.rs** (coordinate_noise function):

Previous code performed multiplication without explicit wrapping:
```rust
.wrapping_add(coord.x as u64 * 0xc2b2ae3d27d4eb4f)
.wrapping_add(coord.y as u64 * 0x165667b19e3779f9)
```

Clippy warning: The `*` operator can overflow even for u64 values when
multiplied by large constants (0xc2b2ae3d27d4eb4f and 0x165667b19e3779f9).
While the .wrapping_add() handles overflow for the addition, the
multiplication itself could panic in debug builds or produce undefined
behavior in release builds if overflow checking is enabled.

## Fix

Wrapped both multiplications explicitly:
```rust
.wrapping_add((coord.x as u64).wrapping_mul(0xc2b2ae3d27d4eb4f))
.wrapping_add((coord.y as u64).wrapping_mul(0x165667b19e3779f9))
```

## Impact

**Functional behavior**: None. In release builds without overflow checks,
the behavior was already wrapping (Rust defaults to wrapping for unsigned
types in release mode). This makes the intent explicit and prevents panics
in debug builds.

**Hash distribution**: Unchanged. The SplitMix64-based hash still produces
the same pseudo-random values for terrain accent noise.

**Performance**: Zero overhead. wrapping_mul() compiles to the same
instruction as unchecked multiplication on all platforms.

## Context

This function generates deterministic noise for terrain accent variation
in the WFC (Wave Function Collapse) map generator. It's called once per
tile during map generation to add visual variety without changing gameplay
properties like elevation or moisture.

The overflow is intentional as part of the hash avalanche process—we
want the bits to mix chaotically. Making the wrapping explicit documents
this intent and satisfies clippy::arithmetic_overflow lint.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
